submission.questionnaireText:
Caity Black
Adult Schedule appointment  Edit

1. Initial Health Survey (complete at your earliest convenience)
09/25/2025 at 7:36 PM
Note



1. Do you gain weight easily?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
2. [IF SO] Where do you typically gain weight on your body?
Uniformly
3. Do you feel "puffy," i.e your fat tends to be soft and feel like water retention?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
4. Do you struggle with unintended weight loss?
(1) I do not experience this
5. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you accumulate cellulite?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
6. Do you struggle with low appetite?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
7. Do you tend to wake up when sleeping?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
8. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it: within the first few hours of falling asleep OR later on in the night OR both?
Both
9. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it to: urinate OR eat something OR drink something OR seemingly no reason?
Seemingly no reason
10. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, do you find that you are hot or sweaty upon waking?
No
11. Do you NOT dream?
(1) I do not experience this
12. Do you experience nightmares or sleep paralysis?
(1) I do not experience this
13. Do you have trouble falling asleep?
(1) I do not experience this
14. Do you feel like you DON'T get a restful sleep?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
15. Do you frequently feel cold, especially at the extremities (fingertips, toes, and nose)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
16. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have painful menstrual cramps?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
17. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have irregular menstruation or suffer from premenstrual syndrome?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
18. Do you suffer from chronic congestion without mucus?
(1) I do not experience this
19. Do you frequently experience mucus buildup in the throat or nose?
(1) I do not experience this
20. [IF SO] Is it exacerbated after eating?
Not Applicable
21. Do you suffer from dental problems such as bad breath, cavities, and gum inflammation?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
22. Do you often find that you are irritable or easily agitated?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
23. Do you suffer from "brain fog," low energy or feel like you do not have mental clarity?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
24. Do you tend to have an "afternoon crash" after eating lunch?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
25. Do you often feel "hyper aware," jittery, or restless?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
26. Do you notice the sensation of your heart pounding or difficulty breathing?
(1) I do not experience this
27. Have you had a loss of pleasure in activities you normally enjoy?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
28. Have you noticed a lack of sexual interest or desire?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
29. [MEN ONLY] Have you lost morning erections?
Not Applicable
30. Do you have skin issues such as dry skin, eczema, or acne?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
31. Can you trace a worsening of your symptoms back to a traumatic event?
No
32. Do you have specific food cravings? If so, please list the foods you crave.
No
33. Do you have a history of mental health issues? If so, please list them.
Yes
I’ve been diagnosed with major depressive disorder, panic disorder, complex post traumatic stress disorder, pure-OCD, generalized anxiety disorder
34. Do you experience constipation?
(1) I do not experience this
35. Do you experience nausea?
(1) I do not experience this
36. Do you experience bloating (subjective feeling)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
37. Do you experience distention (visible expansion of the abdomen)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
38. Do you experience diarrhea?
(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me
39. Do you experience belching?
(1) I do not experience this
40. Do you experience flatulence?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
41. Do you experience pain in your upper abdomen?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
42. Do you experience pain in your right upper quadrant?
(1) I do not experience this
43. Do you experience pain in your lower abdomen?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
44. Are you aware of any specific foods that trigger your symptoms? If so, please list them.
Yes
I have only been “tolerating” a2 milk, white rice, maple syrup, eggs, carnivore Aurelius collagen peptides and stewed apples since around April 2025. I am pretty sure it’s at least histamine related at a minimum but pretty much any other food not on the list is not agreeing with me. It’ll flare up my skin and/or send me running to the bathroom with diarrhea within an hour of eating it.
45. Do you experience dry stools?
(1) I do not experience this
46. Do you experience acid reflux?
(1) I do not experience this
47. Do your symptoms worsen IMMEDIATELY after eating?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
48. Do your symptoms worsen 1-3 hours after eating?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
No
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
I wasn’t a THC (delta 9/CBD edible on combo bought over the counter) user until Oct 2024, that’s when I started using that. Prior to that I had the slight weight gain, lower abdomen puffiness and all over “heavier” feeling (this started sometime early 2024, before any THC usage). It was around Sept 2024 when the rosacea started, but very subtly. Just a warm feeling on occasion but no substantial redness or pustules. By early 2025 I definitely was a “heavier” user of THC and quit cold turkey because I noticed it was making the rosacea worse and I wanted to not be a THC user anymore as I learned how bad it is for you. So that was the first thing I quit, but that’s when the digestive issues right after quitting and it was intense, I didn’t have any digestive issues prior to using THC for many years. At this point in time, almost every food would flare up my face and result in redness and pustules and I was experiencing flatulence for the first time that was not pleasant and diarrhea/loose stools. I thought perhaps my sulfur detox pathways were overburdened for some reason so I began Pure Encapsulations manganese (8mg/day), Pure Encapsulations selenium (200mcg/day), Magnesium Glycinate (200mg/day) and Objective Nutrients Better GI 2x/day. I didn’t notice a difference at all from the Better GI and discontinued use. I stayed on the rest but didn’t notice a major difference in flatulence until adding in Seeking Health Molybdenum 500mcg/day in May 2025. I also added in Pure Encapsulations Zinc Picolinate 15mg/day in May 2025 in hopes of helping my skin and worked up to 30mg/day by the end of May. Around these months is when I adopted “no/low histamine” diet. My chronic body pains were almost alleviated overnight from this and while my skin was far from perfect, I had significantly less flushing. Around this time I tried incorporating b vitamins (Objective Nutrients Thiavite as I can’t tolerate methylated vitamins even though I have the MTHFR gene) and they went right through me. I have tried a tiny portion of 1 capsule and no matter what, it induces diarrhea. Around mid June 2025 I added in 1000mg/day of NOW Foods powdered sodium ascorbate. I did this in preparation for my breast implant explant surgery July 30, 2025 because I wanted to help my body heal as best it could. I also added in Pure Encapsulations Boron glycinate (2mg/day) in June 2025 because I realized my restrictive diet was missing that. I tried Pure TheraPro RX Saccharomyces Bouldarii starting mid July 2025 in hopes of making my digestion even better (I would have mostly normal bowels at this point but seemingly random diarrhea). This probiotic temporarily helped but sometime after surgery in mid Aug, it stopped helping and I discontinued use. I won’t lie I talked to ChatGPT at my wits end to try and troubleshoot and it recommended increasing my carbs and lowering my fat to help my thyroid, digestion, and skin. I aim for around 1800kcals a day getting 100-115g of protein, 230-260g of carbs and no more than 55g of fat (and no lower than 40g). This macro change had a big impact and if I stay within these macros and the restrictive food list, my bowels are completely regular and no flatulence. I am still dealing with the skin issues though and the diet is so restrictive. I also added in 600mg of Life Giving Store’s lactoferrin in July 2025, I can’t say if it did anything but I’m sticking with it as I was told it can help lower or increase ferritin depending on what the body needs and has so many other benefits. I did donate blood in May because my iron/iron saturation was high so I needed to bring that down, but that also brought my ferritin down to 36. The blood donation had no effect on any of my symptoms. I also added in Ancestral Supplements Dessicated Liver (6 capsules/day) as of 3 weeks ago in hopes of getting some b vitamins and skin benefits but I am unsure if it’s helping or not yet. I had to discontinue the zinc and selenium as those both are now causing diarrhea within 30min-1hr of taking them (even with the macro change). I had my explant surgery July 30, 2025 and had perfect skin and bowel movements for about a week, even with eating “bad” food. I am unsure if this was due to the potent acute anti inflammatory effect from the cortisol or what but it was short lived (I was given IV antibiotics during surgery but didn’t take any prescription antibiotics post-op). I also added in BodyBio Liposomal Vitamin C (1000-1500mg/day), I do think it’s brightened my skin a little bit but I’ve only been on it for one week but definitely noticing something compared to the sodium ascorbate. I added in LifeBlud’s Taurine (1500mg/day) about a week ago as well because I heard it’s helpful for anxiety and hair growth, haven’t noticed any negative effects nor changes in symptoms but I do get a slight calming effect
51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
Yes
-life giving store lactoferrin 600mg/day (neutral, don’t know if it’s doing anything but I like the sounds of the benefits)
-forefront health raw desiccated thyroid glandular 130mg 2x/day (this has made me feel much warmer and more energized. I went up to two capsules a day a month ago because I wasn’t happy with my labs on one a day and I do feel warmer and more energized on this dose)
-forefront health micronized Pregnenolone 40mg/day (I really love the sound of this supplement but think I probably need a higher dose. I was hoping it would help with stress, depression and anti-aging/skin)
-pure encapsulations boron glycinate 2mg/day (I take this to fill nutrient gaps I think I have in my diet)
-ancestral supplements beef liver 6 capsules/3000mg a day (anytime I’ve consistently taken this in the past I feel my skin and hair is amazing and I don’t know why I ever stopped. I think it’s too soon to tell right now if it’s going to have the same effect. I also don’t seem to tolerate any B-complex vitamins I’ve tried and I know this is low in B1 but I’m hopeful it’s doing a better job hitting the other B vitamins)
-pure encapsulations vitamin d3 5000 IU/day (I don’t get outside at all so hoping this will help me)
-forefront health vitamin k2 m4 5mg/day (I don’t get this in my diet so taking to fill that gap)
-why not natural magnesium glycinate 420mg elemental magnesium/day (I recently went from 200mg to 420mg the past week and am hoping to see improvements. This is the dose I need to feel sleepy and it definitely helps with sleep)
-forefront health molecular progesterone complex 9-18mg a day on day 14-28 of my menstrual cycle (this usually has a calming effect but I do still suffer from severe depression/PMDD so unsure if I could use a bump in this dosage)
-forefront health thyro-protect vitamin e complex 1-2 pumps a day so 238-475mg of mixed tocopherols (I take this to fill in gaps in my diet, I have heard it can lower estrogen too but unsure if that’s a problem I have or not)
-seeking health molybdenum 500mcg/day (this is what definitely helped my gas forever ago but I recently learned microcrystalline cellulose is bad and this has it so I’m looking for an alternative source)
-I think I absolutely need to supplement zinc and selenium due to my limited diet but again, the doses I was taking at 30mg/day and 200mcg/day started to cause diarrhea so I discontinued recently
-LifeBlud taurine 1500mg/day (I think this is having the calming effect I’d like, curious if a higher dose would help me further as I know 1500mg is on the lower end)
-Bodybio Liposomal vitamin c 1000-1500mg/day (recently added this but I think it’s provided a slight brightening of my skin. I was hoping it would help but I did just start it so too soon to tell. I know I don’t get any vitamin c in my diet either)
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
No
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
No
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
exactly what I eat each day to mitigate symptoms and stay within macros: 6 cups of Alexandre A2 2% milk, 4 organic free range eggs cooked in a tiny bit of organic coconut oil, 52-62g of organic Greenwise maple syrup, 150g of organic white jasmine rice, I sometimes brew Peet’s swiss water method decaf coffee in a stainless steel moka pot and add that to the milk & maple syrup to make a cold latte, 2 scoops of Carnivore Aurelius hydrolyzed collagen powder. Sometimes I have 1-2 medium organic carrots raw as I know that is supposed to be good for endotoxin reduction. I prepare everything myself and use non toxic cookware. I have my first coffee drinks anywhere from 8-10am, lunch a couple of hours later, milk & collagen for a snack a couple of hours later and maybe a coffee drink, dinner around 7-9pm, and then another collagen and milk drink before bed

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
Sadly I don’t go outside anymore (I used to as I have a horse and love being outside) because it flares up my skin really badly. I used to exercise 3-5x/week but had to discontinue post-op. I currently try to brisk walk for 20-30min a day and have added back in lower body workouts 2x/week. I won’t be cleared for full workouts until Nov 2025 due to the surgery. I am extremely stressed almost everyday due to being very lonely and trying to move but feeling too sick to push myself with work to make the money I’d want to in order to move. All of my immediate in person support system have moved away over the past few years and it’s just me left in Florida and I know I need to move to feel better and build a community where I actually want to live but everything is on pause right now due to health. I am a content creator so thankfully I make my own schedule and that helps alleviate work stress as much as possible. Oh, and I do own quality red light panels so I do that 30min a day. I know it’s not the same as sunlight but wanted to mention I have access to those and enjoy them.

56. What are your health primary goals you are looking achieve?
My skin. I want to resolve this rosacea issue and I am 31, almost 32 and kind of obsessed with aging as gracefully as possible. So anything that helps with fine lines, hydration, collagen production, slowing down aging I would love and good hair growth/retention. I’d like to improve my digestion where I can tolerate more foods and get my metabolism as good as it can be. I noticed it feels like my metabolism has slowed down over the past year or so and I’m guessing that is due to my thyroid and whatever other issues. I just don’t have the lower ab definition I used to a year or so ago and feel puffy/heavy and would love to have my abs back. It would be cool if my cellulite could be reduced as well, as I am quite small but also have dealt with cellulite even when I’m in really good shape and since I first hit puberty. Anything that will help with my surgery healing would be great too. Lastly, I am unsure how much of my depression is due to life circumstances vs whatever is wrong with my health but the depression is pretty bad. As well as occasional panic attacks, I’m curious if there are any supplements to help instead of taking klonopin for when these happen

57. Have you ever had food poisoning before? If so, when?
No

58. Please list your height, weight and waist measurements.
5’5 and a half, 118lbs, 24” waist

59. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
I have hereditary hemochromatosis (I have theC282Y and H63D gene, first had high iron levels by the time I was 15 years old). I had Lyme disease when I was four years old as well as shingles. I haven’t had any flare up since that I know of but I feel like being on antibiotics at such a young age maybe did a number on my immune system.
submission.takehomeText:
What are your four resting heart beats per minute? Please separate values with commas. [example: 78, 81, 75, 80]
72, 68, 68, 68

How many times did you urinate each day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 6, 6, 8, 7]
11, 6, 6, 8

On average, how foamy was your urine?
(1) Not foamy at all
How many times did you defecate per day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 3, 1, 0, 2]
2, 2, 1, 3

What Types on the Bristol Stool Scale did each of your bowel movements fall under? Please separate values with commas. [example: Type 2, Type 2, Type 4, Type 5, Type 7, Type 1, Type 6]
Please describe any other abnormalities of the stool observed, such as altered color or undigested food in the stool.
Type 5, type 4, type 4, type 4, type 4, type 4, type 3, type 1

What were both of your temperature measurements each morning? Please separate values with commas. [example:
98.2, 98.4]

96.8, 96.8, 96.8, 96.8

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did your last period begin? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
09/18/2025

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did you take your first temperature measurement? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
09/26/2025
submission.advisorNotesText:
Appointment #1
With: Shannon Sirois 
Billing code: 
consulting
Edit
2:00 PM – 3:00 PM
Progress Note 
I took a few days to research this case, as this was very interesting and she had to do a bit of investigating to get info on the two versions of antibiotics that she took.

Caity is 31 y.o. F that has had a long history of health issues that started when she was a child. She had an episode when she was a kid where she got very sick and her hair was falling out, I believe that she said she was about 15 when this happened but ever prior to that she was very sickly and was on many rounds of antibiotics for different things throughout her life. From this episode at 15 y.o. she was finally dx with hemochromatosis and has two copies of the gene. She’s had on and off health problems since then, and has given blood a few times to help with this but is worried because her ferritin is on the lower end. It’s currently in the 30’s, which I actually think is a healthy range for her. She recently had some blood work (she uploaded) that showed high iron saturation so she went and donated blood and then had a retest (also uploaded and shows 46% after donating). She, however, felt very run down after this donation and it was not helpful.

Since that donation, she has taken matters into her own hands and did start taking liver pill supplements. She did this because despite her efforts with supplements, any B vitamins whether regular or methylated are giving her almost immediate and severe diarrhea. She said if she takes them at all, within 10 min she has diarrhea. B vitamins do it, zinc does it, selenium does it. The few supplements that she’s been ok with so far are taurine and lactoferrin, and most recently the liver supplement.

Her reason for coming to us is her intense intestinal issues. It took me a while to get to the bottom of this, but I think I figured it out. Most of her life, digestive issues were not really a problem even when she was eating a very poor diet. Over the last year plus, she has been battling intense bouts of diarrhea that are triggered by the most random things and that’s why her current diet is so limited (see intake, she was very detailed). After quite a bit of messaging we realized that in late Dec 2024 she had a UTI and took SMZ-TMP, which is a sulfur based antibiotic. This definitely sparked the sx’s she is struggling with. Looking back through pictures, she was able to identify that this is also when her rosacea truly surfaced.

So her second complaint is rosacea. About a month to 2 months after taking this antibiotic her face errupted into pustules, redness, histamine flares, etc. and since then she’s had to be so careful with her diet. Right around this time she was also coming off of eating a very high red meat/animal based diet with her boyfriend at the time (they since broke up about a year ago). The thing I found interesting about that diet was that she said she actually felt amazing while eating it, and had no sx’s. Fast forward to now, and she can’t eat anything like that in the way of red meat/high fat or really many animal products because it will set her off digestively/diarrhea and her skin will errupt.

This is a very interesting case because I have a lot of the same sx’s as she does. I also have hemochromatosis but do not load iron into ferritin. Instead it gets really high in my blood and my saturation goes up and I will feel like absolute garbage until I donate blood. There’s something about the female estrogen profile that I feel like has this genotype storing iron in tissue/blood rather than putting it into storage where it’s safer. I also notice that there is a direct correlation to iron overload, high estrogen and poor sulfur pathways. She seems to fit this exact profile.

I’m a bit perplexed at her response to B vitamins and minerals, though? The only thing I can think of here is that she has some severe dysbiosis going on and these things act as an antimicrobial in a way and she gets an immediate reaction. She did recently have a surgery for a breast explant. During this surgery they gave her vancomycin IV abx. She said that for a week after surgery her digestion was perfect, her face was clear and she was eating anything she wanted. A week after that everything came back with a vengeance. So I’m inclined to think that we are dealing with a sulfur reducing bacterial overgrowth perhaps? I’m pretty sure vancomycin would help that.

Please review her very detailed write-up, she had some interesting thoughts about some of her sx’s.

Diet she’s limited to:
exactly what I eat each day to mitigate symptoms and stay within macros: 6 cups of Alexandre A2 2% milk, 4 organic free range eggs cooked in a tiny bit of organic coconut oil, 52-62g of organic Greenwise maple syrup, 150g of organic white jasmine rice, I sometimes brew Peet’s swiss water method decaf coffee in a stainless steel moka pot and add that to the milk & maple syrup to make a cold latte, 2 scoops of Carnivore Aurelius hydrolyzed collagen powder. Sometimes I have 1-2 medium organic carrots raw as I know that is supposed to be good for endotoxin reduction. I prepare everything myself and use non toxic cookware. I have my first coffee drinks anywhere from 8-10am, lunch a couple of hours later, milk & collagen for a snack a couple of hours later and maybe a coffee drink, dinner around 7-9pm, and then another collagen and milk drink before bed

Goals:

reduce rosacea

Improve diet variety

Stop diarrhea episodes

Tolerance to vitamins/minerals

Her number one goal is her skin. She is so limited and can’t really even go outside because this will flare up her sx’s. I really think if we address her sulfur pathways we can fix this problem.

Iron chelation techniques: I’ve had really good luck using EGCG or grapefruit seed extract here. I think EGCG may be the way to go because it’s also effective against sulfur reducing bacteria. I prefer 1 dose a.m. Designs for Health brand.

Vitamin E: I like lifeblud for iron regulation and estrogen detox.

B vitamins: she’s currently taking liver, but I feel like she needs folate and B2 for iron regulation and thyroid. Let’s try designs for health liquid folate, and any thoughts on the riboflavin? Should we rely on the liver for this since it’s not bothering her currently?

Minerals: I really think she needs some bioavailable copper and zinc to offset some of that iron. Anderson minerals 1/2 dose with her biggest meal would be great here. I don’t think she’s in a place to add mineral rich foods, so I think this may be best for now.

I’m torn with using an antibiotic here. It seems like vancomycin helped quite a bit. Should we try this in our short term low dose regimen?

CoQ10 for sulfur metabolism: I use designs for health, but if we have one with a discount code then that’s fine too!

She’s getting a full iron panel as well, already ordered.

If we do choose to use antibiotics, let’s have her continue with lactoferrin and also add lactobacillus plantarum and lactobacillus rhamnosus one at a time so we can gauge her response.

Thyroid: I do think she would benefit from this. The high iron profile definitely slow thyroid function down. I prefer tyromax
submission.daltonsFinalNotes:
i agree let’s roll w the liver > riboflavin

molybdenum

we can include vancomycin + doxycycline

Saturee - use as directed, for explanation:

“Saturee is one of the most comprehensive anti-aging skin serums on the market, containing a variety of ingredients to strengthen the skin.

Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride
A lightweight emollient derived from coconut oil and glycerin that helps soften skin and improve spreadability. It reinforces the skin barrier and prevents water loss, keeping skin supple and reducing fine-line visibility.

Cholesterol
A natural component of the skin barrier, it restores barrier integrity and improves moisture retention. Maintaining lipid balance helps keep skin resilient and less prone to dryness-induced wrinkling.

Coco-Caprylate/Caprate
A natural silicone alternative that provides a silky texture and enhances spreadability. It reduces transepidermal water loss, helping skin stay smooth and hydrated.

Glyceryl Behenate
Functions as an emollient and thickener, creating a protective layer on the skin. This helps lock in moisture and protects from environmental aggressors that accelerate aging.

Urea
A natural moisturizing factor (NMF) that hydrates by binding water and softening keratin. It smooths rough patches and increases skin elasticity, delaying wrinkle formation.

Niacinamide (Vitamin B3)
Improves skin tone, strengthens the barrier, and boosts collagen production. It reduces fine lines, hyperpigmentation, and dullness associated with aging.

Glycerin
A classic humectant that draws water into the skin, plumping it up. Regular hydration reduces the depth of wrinkles and enhances skin elasticity.

Glyceryl Caprylate
Provides emollient and antimicrobial properties, balancing skin flora. By preventing micro-inflammation, it slows aging processes linked to irritation and barrier breakdown.

Tocopherols (Vitamin E)
A potent antioxidant that protects against free radical damage from UV and pollution. It also stabilizes cell membranes and enhances skin healing, reducing oxidative wrinkle formation.

Caprylhydroxamic Acid
A gentle preservative with antioxidant properties. By keeping formulations stable without irritation, it helps maintain skin health and prevents inflammation-driven aging.

Camphor
Provides a cooling, circulation-stimulating effect that can enhance delivery of other actives. It also has mild antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce skin stress.

Citrus x Aurantium (Bitter Orange) Fruit Extract (Naringenin et al.)
Rich in flavonoids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These protect collagen from breakdown and reduce oxidative stress that accelerates wrinkle formation.

Caffeine
Improves microcirculation, reduces puffiness, and has antioxidant effects. It temporarily firms the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines.

Salicylic Acid
A gentle exfoliant (BHA) that clears dead skin cells and unclogs pores. This promotes cell turnover, keeping skin smoother and more youthful in texture.

Eugenol
A component of clove oil with antioxidant and antimicrobial activity. Helps reduce oxidative damage and inflammation that can speed up skin aging.

Ubiquinone (CoQ10)
A powerful mitochondrial antioxidant that protects against free radical damage in skin cells. It also helps maintain collagen and elastin, reducing wrinkle depth over time.

Copper Tripeptide-1
Stimulates collagen, elastin, and glycosaminoglycan production. It promotes skin repair and remodeling, directly improving firmness and reducing wrinkles.”
